headline: Nvidia Marvell Alliance Extends AI Platform From Data Centers To Telecoms
summary: Nvidia (NasdaqGS:NVDA) announced a US$2b partnership with Marvell focused on connecting Marvell’s custom XPUs and networking products into Nvidia’s NVLink Fusion platform. The agreement extends Nvidia’s AI infrastructure into data centers and telecom networks, including AI powered telecommunications and silicon photonics. The deal also includes work on semi custom AI architectures and AI RAN for 5G and 6G, and involves direct equity investment and ecosystem collaboration. Nvidia has been...

headline: Tech Volatility May Just Be Getting Started. What Jittery Investors Can Do Right Now.
summary: It wasn’t long ago that counting on a mega cap tech stock to rise was a pretty safe bet, but that certainty has been turned on its head this year.  The tech-heavy  index has dropped 5.9% in 2026, partly from the uncertainty due to the Iran war.  A tech selloff isn’t unusual when the market abruptly shifts into risk off mode, said John Belton, portfolio manager at Gabelli Funds.

content.title: Tech Volatility May Just Be Getting Started. What Jittery Investors Can Do Right Now.
content.summary: It wasn’t long ago that counting on a mega cap tech stock to rise was a pretty safe bet, but that certainty has been turned on its head this year.  The tech-heavy  index has dropped 5.9% in 2026, partly from the uncertainty due to the Iran war.  A tech selloff isn’t unusual when the market abruptly shifts into risk off mode, said John Belton, portfolio manager at Gabelli Funds.

headline: Arm (ARM) Enters the Chip Market. Here’s Why I’m Bullish
summary: Arm Holdings’ (ARM) entry into the chip market marks a meaningful shift in its business model. The company has long been known as the quiet power behind modern computing, licensing the central processing unit (CPU) designs that sit inside smartphones, data center chips, autos, and more. The stock is already up around 36% year-to-date, yet I remain bullish on ARM as the company moves beyond collecting royalties on others’ success. headline: 5-Star Analyst Raises Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Forecast as Demand for ‘Hopper and Blackwell AI-Chips Likely to Outstrip Supply’
summary: On Thursday, Nvidia stock (NVDA) received a major vote of confidence from analyst Fang Boon Foo from DBS. Holding a prestigious 5-star analyst rating, Foo is known for his accurate forecasts in the tech sector. In his latest update, he reiterated his Buy rating and raised his price target for Nvidia to $220, up from his previous forecast of $180.
